title: Deterministic replay — process-level time travel debugging
purpose: Time-travel debugging allows stepping forward and backward through a process's execution during replay. This is the killer feature — debug a production crash by replaying the event log and inspecting state at any point.
task: Implement forward/backward stepping and state inspection during replay.

requirements.title: Execution stepping
requirements.spec: Step forward and backward through instructions during replay.
acceptance: step_forward() executes one instruction and records the state snapshot
step_backward() restores the previous state snapshot
State includes: registers, stack, heap (or heap diff), code position
Snapshots are taken at configurable granularity (every N instructions)
Test: step forward 10 instructions, step back 5, verify state at each point

requirements.title: State inspection API
requirements.spec: Inspect process state at any point during replay.
acceptance: inspect_registers() returns current x/y register values as readable terms
inspect_stack() returns stack frames with function names and local variables
Test: pause at a specific instruction, inspect state, verify correctness

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement a GUI debugger — API only (CLI or IDE integration is separate)
SHALL NOT implement concurrent process debugging — single process at a time
SHALL NOT implement heap diffing optimization — full snapshots first
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
